Implementation of Consistent Overhead Byte Stuffing (COBS) encoding with buffer size calculations.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that provides an implementation of the Consistent Overhead Byte Stuffing (COBS) algorithm. It includes necessary standard headers and defines macros to calculate the overhead and maximum buffer size required for COBS encoding. The macro `COBS_OVERHEAD(n)` calculates the offset needed when encoding data in place, while `MAX_SIZE_AFTER_COBS_ENCODING(n)` determines the maximum buffer size needed to store `n` bytes of data after encoding. The function [`cobs_encode`](<#cobs_encode>) is declared to perform the COBS encoding, taking a source buffer and its length as input, and writing the encoded data to a destination buffer. The destination buffer must be large enough to accommodate the encoded data, as specified by the `MAX_SIZE_AFTER_COBS_ENCODING` macro.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stddef.h`
- `stdint.h`
